## 1. Brief Market Context

The AI meeting-assistant market has matured rapidly through 2024–2025. Products now offer real-time transcription, speaker diarization, LLM-based summarization, and integration with major video platforms. For a small research lab (12 members) using Zoom and Google Meet, the key differentiators are: (a) platform-native vs. standalone bot architecture, (b) per-seat vs. per-meeting pricing, (c) data retention and consent controls, and (d) export flexibility. Budget sensitivity and privacy concerns (research data, consent requirements) narrow the viable options.

## 2. Comparison Matrix

| Product | Standalone or Integrated | Supported Meeting Platforms | Key Features | Public Pricing / Plan Notes | Privacy or Data-Retention Notes | Best Fit | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Otter.ai** | Standalone (bot joins) | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Cisco Webex | Real-time transcription, speaker ID, action-item extraction, searchable transcripts, export (PDF, TXT, SRT, CSV) | **Pro plan**: ~$16.99/user/month (billed annually) – 6,000 min/month, advanced search, export. **Business**: ~$30/user/month – admin console, SSO, priority support. Free tier: 300 min/month, 30 min/meeting. | Data stored on Otter servers; users can delete transcripts. No end-to-end encryption for live transcription. GDPR compliance claimed. Consent: bot announces recording. Retention: indefinite unless manually deleted. | Labs needing rich export options and searchable archive; budget allows ~$200/month for 12 Pro seats. | Bot-join model can feel intrusive; per-user pricing scales linearly; no native Zoom/Meet integration (bot must be invited). |
| **Fireflies.ai** | Standalone (bot joins) | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex, RingCentral, others | Transcription, speaker ID, action-item extraction, topic tracking, search, CRM integrations, Soundbites clips | **Pro plan**: ~$18/user/month (billed annually) – unlimited transcription, AI summaries, search. **Business**: ~$29/user/month – analytics, custom vocabulary. Free tier: limited. | Data stored on Fireflies servers; SOC 2 Type II certified; GDPR compliant. Consent: bot announces recording. Retention: configurable auto-deletion (30/60/90 days or indefinite) on Business plan. | Labs wanting CRM-like action tracking and configurable retention; budget ~$216/month for 12 Pro seats. | Bot-join model; per-user cost; some users report accuracy issues with heavy accents. |
| **Zoom AI Companion** | Integrated (native to Zoom) | Zoom only (native); no Google Meet support | Real-time transcription, smart chapters, summarization, action-item extraction, whiteboard summarization, chat summarization | **Included at no extra cost** for paid Zoom accounts (Pro, Business, Enterprise). Zoom Pro: ~$15.99/host/month. Lab would need 1 host license + 11 participants (free with host). | Data processed within Zoom's infrastructure; Zoom claims no training on customer audio/video without consent. Consent: host controls recording notice. ## 1. Brief Market Context

The AI meeting-assistant market has matured rapidly since 2023, with tools now offering real-time transcription, speaker diarization, LLM-generated summaries, and action-item extraction. The market segments into platform-integrated solutions (e.g., Zoom AI Companion, Google's Duet AI/Workspace features) and standalone third-party tools (e.g., Otter.ai, Fireflies.ai, Fathom, Tactiq). For a small university research lab with 12 members, budget sensitivity, and privacy/consent concerns, the choice involves tradeoffs between cost, feature depth, and data governance.

## 2. Comparison Matrix

| Product | Standalone or Integrated | Supported Meeting Platforms | Key Features | Public Pricing / Plan Notes | Privacy or Data-Retention Notes | Best Fit | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Zoom AI Companion** | Integrated (Zoom-native) | Zoom only | Real-time transcription, smart summaries, action-item extraction, chat compose | Included at no extra cost for paid Zoom accounts (Business, Enterprise, Education). Zoom One Pro ($149.90/yr/host) may qualify. | Zoom processes meeting data; opt-in consent required per meeting. Data retention policies: Zoom retains AI Companion data per customer's data retention settings. Not used for training without consent. | Labs already on paid Zoom plans | Only works within Zoom; no Google Meet support; limited export flexibility |
| **Google Workspace (Duet AI / "Help me write" & Gemini in Meet)** | Integrated (Google Workspace-native) | Google Meet only | AI-generated summaries, auto-recorded transcripts, speaker labels, action items | Included with Google Workspace Business Plus ($18/user/mo) or Enterprise. Education Fundamentals may have limited access. | Google's AI features process meeting data; data used per Workspace terms. Admins control retention

1. Brief Market Context

The AI meeting-assistant market has matured rapidly through 2024–2025, with tools now offering real-time transcription, speaker diarization, LLM-generated summaries, and action-item extraction. The market segments into platform-integrated solutions (native to Zoom or Google Meet) and standalone third-party tools (bot-join models like Otter.ai, Fireflies.ai, and Fathom). For a small university research lab with 12 members using Zoom and Google Meet, the key tradeoffs are: per-seat pricing vs. included features, data governance and consent controls, export flexibility, and whether a third-party bot is acceptable given research privacy requirements.

2. Comparison Matrix

| Product | Standalone or Integrated | Supported Meeting Platforms | Key Features | Public Pricing / Plan Notes | Privacy or Data-Retention Notes | Best Fit | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Zoom AI Companion** | Integrated (native to Zoom) | Zoom only | Real-time transcription, smart chapters, meeting summaries, action-item extraction, chat compose, whiteboard summarization | Included at no extra cost for paid Zoom accounts (Pro, Business, Enterprise, Education). Zoom One Pro: ~$149.90/year/host (1 host license supports unlimited participants). | Data processed within Zoom's infrastructure; Zoom states it does not use customer audio/video/transcripts to train AI models without consent. Host controls recording notice. Transcript retention follows Zoom Cloud Recording settings (configurable 1–365 days). | Labs already using paid Zoom; zero additional cost; strongest privacy (no third-party bot). | Google Meet not supported; no standalone export of notes (only via Zoom Cloud Recording); speaker ID limited to Zoom user names. |
| **Google Meet Gemini (formerly Duet AI)** | Integrated (native to Google Meet) | Google Meet only | "Take notes for me" feature, real-time transcription, auto-summarization, action-item extraction, searchable in Google Docs | Included with Google Workspace add-on: Gemini Business (~$24/user/month) or Gemini Enterprise (~$30/user/month). Also available with some Workspace plans (Business Plus at $18/user/month may include limited AI features). | Google processes meeting data per Workspace terms; admins control data retention policies. Google states customer data is not used to train AI models without explicit consent. | Labs already on Google Workspace; seamless Docs integration. | Zoom not supported; requires paid Gemini add-on for full features; per-user cost adds up (~$288/month for 12 users on Gemini Business). |
| **Otter.ai** | Standalone (bot joins) | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Cisco Webex | Real-time transcription, speaker ID, action-item extraction, searchable transcripts, export (PDF, TXT, SRT, CSV, DOCX) | Pro plan: ~$16.99/user/month (billed annually) – 6,000 min/month, advanced search, export. Business: ~$30/user/month – admin console, SSO, priority support. Free tier: 300 min/month, 30 min/meeting limit. | Data stored on Otter servers; users can delete transcripts. Otter claims GDPR compliance. Bot announces recording at join. Retention: indefinite unless manually deleted; no auto-deletion on Pro plan. | Labs needing rich export options and searchable archive; budget ~$204/month for 12 Pro seats. | Bot-join model may feel intrusive for research discussions; per-user pricing scales linearly; no native integration (bot must be invited to each meeting). |
| **Fireflies.ai** | Standalone (bot joins) | Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex, RingCentral, others | Transcription, speaker ID, action-item extraction, topic tracking, search, CRM integrations, Soundbites clips, custom vocabulary | Pro plan: ~$18/user/month (billed annually) – unlimited transcription, AI summaries, search. Business: ~$29/user/month – analytics, custom vocabulary, auto-deletion retention settings. Free tier: limited. | SOC 2 Type II certified; GDPR compliant. Bot announces recording. Business plan offers configurable auto-deletion (30/60/90 days or indefinite). Data stored on Fireflies servers. | Labs wanting configurable data retention and CRM-like action tracking; budget ~$216/month for 12 Pro seats. | Bot-join model; per-user cost; some users report accuracy issues with technical/scientific vocabulary. |

3. Market Trends and Differentiators

- **Platform-integrated vs. standalone:** Zoom and Google are embedding AI natively, eliminating third-party data exposure and bot-join friction. This is a major advantage for privacy-sensitive settings like university research.
- **Pricing convergence:** Standalone tools charge $16–$30/user/month, while integrated tools are either included (Zoom) or require a premium add-on (Google Gemini). For a 12-person lab, Zoom AI Companion is the only zero-additional-cost option.
- **Export and search:** Standalone tools (Otter, Fireflies) offer richer export formats and cross-platform search. Integrated tools tie data to their own cloud ecosystems.
- **Consent and compliance:** All tools provide bot-announcement or host-controlled recording notices. However, third-party bots introduce an additional data processor, which may require a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) under university policy.

4. Recommendation for the 12-Member Lab

**Primary recommendation: Zoom AI Companion** (if the lab primarily uses Zoom). It is included at no extra cost with a paid Zoom account, requires no third-party data sharing, and provides transcription, summaries, and action-item extraction. The lab needs only one paid host license (~$149.90/year) for all 12 members.

**Secondary recommendation: Otter.ai Pro** (if the lab needs Google Meet support and rich export). At ~$204/month for 12 seats, it offers the best export flexibility and searchable transcripts across both Zoom and Google Meet.

**If the lab uses Google Meet exclusively and already has Google Workspace:** Google Meet Gemini is the most seamless option, but at ~$288/month for 12 users on Gemini Business, it is the most expensive integrated option.

5. Risks, Assumptions, and Verification Notes

- **Pricing accuracy:** Pricing figures are based on publicly available information as of early 2025. Actual pricing may vary by region, educational discounts, or negotiated contracts. University labs may qualify for education pricing not reflected here.
- **Privacy and consent:** All standalone tools (Otter, Fireflies) require a bot to join meetings, which may conflict with university IRB or data-governance policies regarding third-party access to research discussions. The lab should verify whether a DPA is required.
- **Data retention:** Otter.ai's Pro plan does not offer configurable auto-deletion; Fireflies.ai offers this only on the Business plan. Zoom AI Companion retention follows the lab's Zoom Cloud Recording settings.
- **Feature parity:** Zoom AI Companion does not support Google Meet. If the lab uses both platforms equally, a standalone tool (Otter or Fireflies) may be necessary, adding cost and privacy considerations.
- **Verification:** Pricing and feature details were sourced from official product pages and reputable technology review sites. Specific educational discount programs were not verified and may be available.

6. Source List

2. Zoom One Pro pricing. Zoom Pricing Page. https://zoom.us/pricing (accessed 2025).
3. Otter.ai pricing plans. Otter.ai. https://otter.ai/pricing (accessed 2025).
4. Otter.ai features and platform support. Otter.ai Help Center. https://help.otter.ai (accessed 2025).
5. Fireflies.ai pricing plans. Fireflies.ai. https://fireflies.ai/pricing (accessed 2025).
6. Fireflies.ai security and compliance. Fireflies.ai Trust Center. https://fireflies.ai/trust (accessed 2025).
7. Google Workspace Gemini add-on pricing. Google Workspace. https://workspace.google.com/pricing (accessed 2025).
8. Google Meet "Take notes for me" feature. Google Workspace Updates Blog. https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/google-meet-take-notes-for-me (accessed 2025).
